review_text: In my opinion, this is the greatest film ever made. With an amazing director to these actors, amazing acting from Matt Damon and Robin Williams, who carry this movie with their amazing chemistry and story. The story is great, with its themes of abandonment, trauma, and taking the risk of letting people into our lives. Over all, Good Will Hunting gets better and better after every rewatch, and will probably remain my favourite movie for a long time.

This movie was just… excellent. I’ve never in my life written a movie review. This movie was inspirational, relatable, and sensitive in all the right ways. As someone who has been through similar experiences to Will in the movie and struggles with those same things it really touched me on a personal level. This is now by far my favorite movie. I will never forget it.

Good Will Hunting gives us insight into human behavior. The protagonist carries a mountain of personal issues, hides them, refuses honesty, and avoids real connection. He lacks self-control, disregards others’ feelings, and lies constantly. Watching him for long stretches is exhausting. He offers fleeting moments of sympathy, but remains impossible to view romantically. Others’ expectations of him are wasted. The film focuses on healing and growth. If the protagonist cannot evolve, nothing changes.

There is so much i could say about this film. If you don't know the plot, the story follows Will Hunting, a genius who acts like a complete dumbass. But this movie takes what any other film would make a comedy, and turns it into some of the deepest shit I've ever seen. The fact that they can introduce a whole set of characters, make you understand their inner struggles, solve their problems, and have you care for these characters, all in 2 hours without anything feeling 'rushed', this is one of the first times i've genuinely had to review a movie on here. Because this old classic, changed my life. Robbin Williams is a godsend in this, you know so little yet so much about his character, and yet he gets you to STILL feel every emotion he portrays. I wont spoil the rest of this, because i genuinely believe you should watch this. I'd see it in theaters even (if it ever gets a rerelease). 10/10.

Good Will Hunting is definitely a movie that still delivers the same emotional impact as it did when it was released. A story of a genius janitor is a headline that might get your attention, however, the film is mostly about pain, healing, and getting to know that love and help are not for people who deserve it but even for the ones who think they don’t. Will, a clever yet emotionally lacking guy from South Boston is the main character whose life we follow. The main thing that makes this movie different from others is its plausibility. It doesn’t glamorize any aspect or flaunt with extreme stunts but rather it brings the real people and their real problems to the forefront. One of the best performances of Matt Damon is probably his role as Will. He does not only act but rather feels like one he is the real thing, the brilliance, the anger, and the fear. There is a very strong idea that behind his tough appearance more things are lurking and that’s the reason why he is so much like the human himself in terms of identification. Robin Williams, one of my all time favorites, in the role of Sean the therapist is the one who basically dominates every single scene he is in. The way he slowly but surely wins Will’s trust and then gets him to talk is very credible. Probably the “It’s not your fault” scene is the warmest moment of any film and my favorite scene. The script which was written by Matt Damon and Ben Affleck very much resembles real life and looks like it was done spontaneously. In fact, the characters' dialogues in a movie are the manner in which real people say and not actors who have memorized the script. The film decides to be quiet without using it as a hint for more deep ideas. When Sean is telling Will on the park bench about life and love, it is the finest example of how life away from books is so much more than just getting knowledge from them. It’s one of those things that don’t leave your mind. The place and the music are quite supportive to the film too. The Boston location gives the film a gritty kind of authentic feel while Elliott Smith’s music is perfect for the film. The songs are soft but very emotional and are in absolute harmony with the quiet parts of the movie. The factor that actually makes Good Will Hunting an ageless piece is the message contained in it. This movie serves as a warning that being intelligent is almost worthless if you lack the courage to face your past or to share with others. It's a very strong and inspiring message, especially the part that shows Will choosing his life-changing path all by himself ironically at the end. This movie would be different from all other movies if its authenticity alone. It doesn’t try to be what it is not. It’s sad and both funny, while being easily relatable. The kind of story that keeps making you think about your own life long after you have watched it. -Luke Getz

Been so many years since I last saw this superb film. Robin Williams was a genius on screen. Listening to his words you believed his pain and perhaps it now takes on more poignancy given we know how that ended for him. There are certain scenes that just stayed with you through the decades. The bar scene where Will confronts the arrogant Harvard students, Robin Williams describing the night he first met his wife are just two. Ben & Matt definitely hit it out of the park when it came to this script and backed it up with their performances as did all the cast. A beautiful film and I now feel I need to rewatch Dead Poets Society as that’s been too long since I last saw that.

“Good Will Hunting” features outstanding acting and richly developed characters. Matt Damon and Robin Williams deliver powerful, heartfelt performances that elevate the film. The story is both compelling and deeply engaging, drawing the viewer in from start to finish. Overall, the movie stands out as a true masterpiece and personal favorite. Its profound message about life is both enriching and transformative.

A masterpiece about friendships, Therapy, potential, and the beauty in imperfections. Coming from the minds of Ben Affleck and Matt Damon, Good Will Hunting is a deep dive into what it means to be a unique individual in a world full of imperfections. From the rivalry of two great minds, one choosing greatness while the other choosing passion and love. It delves into a great mind that can see calculations without even knowing anything about them. It takes great inspiration from the historical figures like Beethoven and Tesla that saw something no mere human could ever! With the exploration of the Human mind also comes the broken parts that are the focus of this film. It discusses very human topics like broken minds, healing, loss, and imperfections. Every single actor did a great job playing their parts, but Matt Damon with Robin Williams genuinely brought tears to my eyes. Seeing their chemistry, both the greatest minds being tested but life and lost, is something I have never really seen in a film being discussed this beautifully. I honestly loved the metaphorical dialogues and relationships between the characters and the subtle roles they play in each other’s lives. The writing is just beautiful and heartwarming, and seeing Robin Williams talk about emotions never gets old. No one is perfect, but what matters is if you find your perfect friend/partner, the one who sees what you don’t see in yourself. Just like how Matt saw calculations as child’s play but couldn’t see his own broken mind, where others could sense it so clearly. PROS: - Emotional Deep writing. - Robin Williams’ brilliance. - Great dialogues and chemistry. - Huge rewatch value. CONS: - Honestly couldn’t find any. It is generally a really good film. SCORE: 10/10

I love this film. It is such a classic. As someone who has experienced trauma they explore this topic and how it alters people’s behaviours and relationships towards others. It’s a beautiful tale of Sean and Will both who are struggling to make connections and struggling with expressing emotions. Sean because of his wives recent passing and Will because he feels he is undeserving. Honestly this was well written. I enjoy Skylars character as she portrays an outsider who wants to someday understand Will. She is willing to try and help him see that he is deserving of love. And I enjoy the end of the film when Will finally has the courage to give the ultimate FU to his past abusers and seek what he wants for himself because he can. It’s the ultimate self healing film and whenever I get bad when thinking of my own traumas this film helps me reminds me that we are all deserving of love and to try with our relationships because we owe it to ourselves to try.
